description: Local parameter shadowing in BNRG.transferFrom: the parameter named `owner` hides the inherited `Ownable.owner()` function name inside this function scope. This does not by itself create the restricted-mode DoS exploit, but it makes ownership-related code easier to misread and can confuse manual review or tooling when reasoning about the caller, token owner, and contract owner.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in transferFrom
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Local parameter shadowing in BNRG.allowance: the `owner` parameter shadows the inherited `Ownable.owner()` function name. The function is a read-only allowance lookup, so the direct security impact is low, but the naming collision can obscure whether code is referring to an ERC20 token holder or the privileged contract owner.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in allowance
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Local parameter shadowing in BNRG.boosterBuyingAllowance(address,address): the `owner` parameter shadows `Ownable.owner()`. The function checks booster-buying delegation for a token holder, but the reused name can make review of privileged ownership logic less clear.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in two-argument boosterBuyingAllowance
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Local parameter shadowing in BNRG.boosterBuyingAllowance(address): the `owner` parameter shadows `Ownable.owner()`. The one-argument helper forwards to the two-argument overload with `msg.sender`; the naming collision is low impact but reduces clarity around token-owner versus contract-owner semantics.

locations.label: shadowing parameter in one-argument boosterBuyingAllowance
shadowed Ownable.owner function

description: Missing ownership-transfer event: Ownable.transferOwnership updates `_owner` but does not emit an `OwnershipTransferred` event. Off-chain monitoring, dashboards, and incident response tooling may miss ownership changes even though access control state changes successfully.

locations.label: ownership update without event emission

description: Missing zero-address validation in BNRG.setStakingContract: the owner can set `stakingContractAddress` to `address(0)`. Because `deliver` later requires `msg.sender == stakingContractAddress`, setting the zero address effectively disables legitimate staking reward delivery until corrected.

locations.label: unchecked staking contract assignment
locations.contract_content:
141:     function setStakingContract(address stakingContractAddress_) public onlyOwner {
142:         stakingContractAddress = stakingContractAddress_;

locations.label: staking contract address used as authorization gate
